articles.title: Ireland scrapes past Wales in Dublin to stay in Six Nations title race
articles.original_title: Ireland scrapes past Wales in Dublin to stay in Six Nations title race
articles.summary: Ireland scraped past Wales 27-17 and stayed in the Six Nations title race on Friday for at least 24 hours.  France can successfully retain the title by beating Scotland at Murrayfield on Saturday.  While Ireland won its first-ever Friday home championship match with a four-try bonus point, Wales took most of the plaudits.

articles.title: Napoli beats Torino 2-1 to extend unbeaten home run and close gap on second-place AC Milan
articles.original_title: Napoli beats Torino 2-1 to extend unbeaten home run and close gap on second-place AC Milan
articles.summary: Napoli beat Torino 2-1 on Friday to close the gap on second-place AC Milan to one point and maintain its record as the only team still unbeaten at home in Serie A this season.  Torino hasn’t beaten Napoli in the top tier since the 2008-09 season and it fell behind after six minutes.  Alisson Santos created a meter of space on the edge of the Torino penalty area and fired a low shot past the outstretched arm of Alberto Paleari.

articles.title: MiLaysia Fulwiley scores 22, No. 6 LSU blows out No. 7 Oklahoma 112-78 in SEC Tournament
articles.original_title: MiLaysia Fulwiley scores 22, No. 6 LSU blows out No. 7 Oklahoma 112-78 in SEC Tournament
articles.summary: MiLaysia Fulwiley had 22 points and eight assists, Flau’jae Johnson scored 16 of her 21 points in the second half and No. 6 LSU ran away from seventh-ranked Oklahoma 112-78 on Friday in the Southeastern Conference Tournament for its fifth straight victory.  LSU had the second-highest point total in SEC Tournament history, behind only Tennessee’s 118 in 1980.  Mikaylah Williams scored 14 points and Jada Richard had 13 for the Tigers (27-4), who held the Sooners to 36% shooting from the field and

articles.title: PSG drops points in title race after 3-1 home loss to Monaco, opening door for Lens to close gap
articles.original_title: PSG drops points in title race after 3-1 home loss to Monaco, opening door for Lens to close gap
articles.summary: Paris Saint-Germain dropped points in the Ligue 1 title race after losing 3-1 at home to a slick Monaco side on Friday.  Leader PSG's fourth league defeat of the campaign could have been even heavier and rival Lens can move within one point at the top by beating rock-bottom Metz on Sunday.  Livewire winger Maghnes Akliouche opened the scoring for Monaco and American forward Folarin Balogun sealed the win with a deflected strike in the 73rd minute.

articles.title: Achilles healed: Celtics OK Tatum for Fri. return
articles.original_title: Achilles healed: Celtics OK Tatum for Fri. return
articles.summary: Jayson Tatum is officially available for the Celtics' Friday night home game against the Mavericks. Tatum suffered a torn right Achilles tendon in the playoffs less than 10 months ago.

articles.title: Suns forward Brooks arrested on suspected DUI
articles.original_title: Suns forward Brooks arrested on suspected DUI
articles.summary: Suns forward Dillon Brooks was arrested early Friday morning for suspicion of driving under the influence in Scottsdale, Arizona, police said.

articles.title: LeBron bests Kareem for most made FG in history
articles.original_title: LeBron bests Kareem for most made FG in history
articles.summary: LeBron James made the 15,838th field goal of his career to pass Kareem Abdul-Jabbar for the most made field goals in NBA history.
